paragraphs: Rachel D.K. Clark is a contemporary folk artist and educator whose work is rooted in generations of family sewing traditions that began with her great-grandmother. She has been creating wearable art since 1973 and teaching on the national circuit since 1990. Her work blends eclectic fabric combinations, vibrant colors, and a keen sense of humor.

Rachel began quilting as a child and continued sewing through high school, guided by her aunt and early home economics classes. While her garments and quilts can be whimsical, political, or deeply personal, her work always reflects a deep respect for traditional quilt making.

Over the years, Rachel's work has been widely exhibited and featured across the country. She has presented numerous one-woman shows and has been featured in galleries, publications such as Threads and The Quilting Quarterly, at the Bernina Fashion Show, and on HGTV's Simply Quilts.

Rachel is also the designer behind RDKC Patterns, a collection created to support and inspire other makers. Her designs include coats, vests, and quilt patterns, along with a range of instructional booklets that share her techniques and creative approach.
Having ended her affair with beige and chartreuse, Rachel has since embraced turquoise and vintage patterns. She continues to develop her series, Out of the Crayon Box.
